name: StubHub Secondary Ticket Market REST API
what_it_does: StubHub secondary ticket market REST API for ticket sellers, brokers, technology partners, and event industry platforms to list, manage, and sell tickets on StubHub's global secondary ticket marketplace — enabling automated ticket listing, price management, inventory synchronization, order management, and sales analytics through StubHub's marketplace serving millions of buyers across thousands of events globally. Enables AI agents to manage ticket listing for event ticket inventory publishing automation, handle price optimization for dynamic pricing and market rate automation, access inventory for ticket availability and quantity management automation, retrieve order for ticket sale and fulfillment status automation, manage event for upcoming event catalog discovery automation, handle seller for seller account and performance automation, access category for sports, music, and theatre event classification automation, retrieve market data for price benchmarking and demand intelligence automation, manage venue for seating map and section data automation, and integrate StubHub with ticket inventory management, pricing tools, and broker platforms for secondary ticket market automation.
use_cases: Listing and managing ticket inventory for professional ticket broker operations automation agents
Optimizing ticket prices based on market demand for dynamic pricing automation agents
Synchronizing ticket inventory across multiple resale platforms for broker operations automation agents
Monitoring sales performance and market trends for ticket investment analytics automation agents
not_for: Primary ticket sales (StubHub is secondary market resale, not primary box office)
Event venue management (StubHub is marketplace for resale, not venue operations platform)
Ticket manufacturing or printing (StubHub is digital marketplace, not ticket production)
best_when: A ticket broker, resale platform, or sports/entertainment company wanting AI agents to list tickets, optimize prices, manage inventory, and track sales performance on StubHub's secondary ticket marketplace.
avoid_when: SELLER ACCOUNT REQUIRED: StubHub API requires approved seller account; automated open API access assumption creates seller_registration_required; automated must complete StubHub seller verification and approval. TICKET DELIVERY REQUIREMENTS VARY: StubHub delivery methods (mobile transfer, e-ticket, paper) vary by event and venue; automated single-delivery assumption creates delivery_method_mismatch; automated must support multiple delivery methods per event requirements. SELLER FEES APPLY: StubHub charges seller fees on each sale; automated fee-free resale assumption creates unexpected fee deduction; automated must account for StubHub seller fees in pricing strategy. REGION-SPECIFIC PLATFORMS: StubHub operates differently in different markets (StubHub International vs. US); automated uniform global platform assumption creates regional_api_difference; automated must verify API availability for specific target markets.

auth.notes: StubHub uses OAuth2 for marketplace REST API. REST API with JSON. San Francisco, CA HQ. Founded 2000 by Eric Baker and Jeff Fluhr. Owned by Viagogo since 2020. Products: Buyer search, seller listing, pricing tools, inventory management, order management, analytics. SDKs: None official. Largest secondary ticket marketplace in North America. Competes with SeatGeek and Vivid Seats for secondary market dominance.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Sandbox for approved partners; production requires seller account
pricing.paid_tiers: Seller fees per completed sale (typically 10-15%); buyer fees on each purchase; no API fees
pricing.notes: San Francisco CA. Viagogo owned. Per-transaction seller fees. Secondary market leader. Millions of events.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth token; listing and inventory APIs throttled per seller tier

known_agent_gotchas: INVENTORY SYNCHRONIZATION IS CRITICAL: High-demand event tickets can sell quickly; automated delayed inventory sync assumption creates oversell risk from stale inventory; automated must implement real-time inventory updates or conservative listing quantities

PRICE MUST MEET FLOOR: StubHub enforces minimum price floors per event; automated any-price assumption creates below_floor_price_error; automated must query event price floors before setting listing prices

TICKET DELIVERY IS EVENT-SPECIFIC: Some events require specific delivery methods (mobile transfer only, no paper); automated flexible delivery assumption creates delivery_method_required for events with forced delivery type; automated must check event delivery requirements

VIAGOGO MERGER AFFECTS STRATEGY: StubHub is now owned by Viagogo; automated independent StubHub API assumption creates potential Viagogo/StubHub integration overlap; automated should monitor Viagogo/StubHub integration roadmap for API changes

ANTI-SCALPING LAWS VARY: Ticket resale regulations vary by jurisdiction (some require price caps or disclosure); automated regulatory-free resale assumption creates compliance_risk in regulated markets; automated must implement jurisdiction-specific compliance for ticket resale regulations
agent_readiness.security_notes: Secondary ticket marketplace. PCI-DSS, GDPR. OAuth2. US/EU. Ticket and payment transaction data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Ticket marketplace. PCI-DSS, GDPR. OAuth2. US/EU. Ticket and payment data.
